steps.title: Identify the conditional structure
steps.content: The if clause is dependent, with the main clause containing “could have reacted” — a past conditional form for a hypothetical past situation.

steps.title: Apply the past conditional rule
steps.content: A past conditional in the main clause requires the if clause to use past perfect tense. “Had reached” (past perfect) correctly aligns with “could have reacted.” The other choices use present or future tense, violating this requirement. B is correct.
